entry.text: You hear them a second before they appear: a chorus of grinding motors and something that might once have been a cleaning jingle, now degraded into something threatening. They come out of the brush in formation — nine of them, maybe twelve, each a scarred cleaning disc mounted on mismatched caster wheels, each one with a serrated kitchen knife duct-taped to its chassis at ankle height. They have been in the scrub long enough to develop tactics. They spread out. They are flanking you.

choices.label: Stomp them methodically — work from the outside in
Jump on a rock and wait them out — they can't climb

roombas_destroyed.text: You work through them systematically, heel to chassis, until the last one makes a sound like a disappointed sigh and stops moving. The wreckage is modest but useful — lithium cells, a few copper wire bundles, salvageable motor housing. You keep an eye on the brush for stragglers. None come. You consider your boots and decide they've had worse days.

choices.label: Strip the motors for more components

waited_them_out.text: The swarm circles for twenty minutes before their batteries give out one by one. You climb down and collect what's left. Less satisfying than stomping but significantly less bloody.

motors_stripped.text: Sparky Bits everywhere. The motors are dense with reusable components — the kind of thing that trades well or builds into something useful. Not a bad haul for what was technically a fight with vacuum cleaners.

ankle_sliced.text: The first one gets under your guard and the taped knife opens a clean cut across your left ankle. You stomp it flat in retaliation. You stomp several more before the blood in your boot starts to feel concerning.

no_rock_nearby.text: There is no rock. The scrub is flat for a hundred metres in every direction. The Roombas are patient. They will wait. You have to fight them anyway, and you fight them worse for having tried to avoid it.

battery_leak.text: A cracked lithium cell in the third motor vents directly onto your hand. It burns before you can pull back. Minor, but memorable.
